goal: Build a multi-station assembly line in Isaac Sim: a cube spawns on conveyor 1, is carried to robot 1's pick zone, robot 1 picks it up and places it on conveyor 2, conveyor 2 carries it to robot 2, robot 2 picks it up and drops it in a bin. Verified end-to-end with target_source='curobo' on two robots that coexist via per-robot subscription names. Pure tool calls — no run_usd_script.
thoughts:
Twelve load-bearing design choices for multi-robot assembly verified end-to-end with Anton 2026-05-06:
1) Two robots installed via two setup_pick_place_controller calls. The cuRobo handler scopes its physics-step subscription and scene-reset hook by ROBOT_PATH (e.g. _curobo_pp_sub_World_Franka1) so a second install does NOT tear down the first robot's controller.
2) Same Cube_1 listed in both robots' source_paths. Each robot has its own S['delivered'] set; cube enters Robot 1's delivered when it lands on Conv2 (Robot 1's destination_path), but is still un-delivered for Robot 2.
3) Robot 1's destination_path = '/World/Conv2'. _is_in_bin checks cube xy against Conv2's bbox; cube dropped on Conv2 → returns True → marked delivered for Robot 1.
4) Robot 1's drop_target passed EXPLICITLY as [-1.0, -0.4, 0.94]. Conv2's bbox center xy=(0,-0.4) is 1.08m from Robot 1 base at (-1, 0) → out of 0.70m reach → planning fails forever. Explicit drop_target lands the cube within reach AT Conv2's input edge.
5) Drop z=0.94 means panda_hand at 0.94, fingertips at 0.94-0.105=0.835, cube center at 0.835 (held), released to fall onto Conv2 top at z=0.805. Clearance 1cm — industrial sweet spot (5-15mm typical for cube-on-belt; <5mm risks finger-sweep contact, >50mm risks bounce).
6) Robot 1 oriented [0.7071, 0, 0, 0.7071] (90° around Z) so Conv1 at world +Y is in robot's natural +X base direction (matches cuRobo _DOWN_Q_BASE constant).
7) Robot 2 oriented [0.7071, 0, 0, -0.7071] (-90° around Z) so Conv2 at world -Y is in its natural +X base direction. Bin at world +Y is in robot's base -X (drops behind itself, joint 1 swings 180°).
8) Two parallel conveyors at y=+0.4 and y=-0.4, both carrying +X. Robot 1 picks at (-1, +0.4) and drops at (-1, -0.4). Robot 2 picks at (+1, -0.4) and drops at bin (+1, +0.4). Robots are 2m apart (-1 vs +1) so neither's reach overlaps the other's pick zone.
9) CPU dynamics for PhysxSurfaceVelocityAPI (set_physics_scene_config enable_gpu_dynamics=False, broadphase=MBP).
10) sleepThreshold=0 on cube via apply_api_schema(PhysxRigidBodyAPI) + bulk_set_attribute. Without this PhysX deactivates resting cubes when belt is paused, surface velocity ignores sleeping bodies → cube stuck.
11) Rubber physics material on cube (apply_physics_material, material_name='rubber'). PhysX combines friction between contact pairs; rubber-cube against default-mu finger gives ~0.75 effective. Keeps grip reliable without binding material to fingers (which fires PhysX collision-mesh warnings on the parent Xform).
12) cuRobo's settling state (8 ticks) handles the friction-stop after _pause_belt — same fix as CP-01. Mid-height waypoints in _build_segments force vertical descent. pre_grip_settle=0.8s only on grip segments so transit flows continuously.
code:
# Light + Ground (foundational scene)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# Wide table covering both robot stations
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[1.5, 0.5, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# CPU dynamics required for PhysxSurfaceVelocityAPI
set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

# Robot 1 — picks from Conv1 (y=+0.4), drops on Conv2 (y=-0.4)
# Orientation 90° around Z so Conv1 at world +Y is robot's base +X
robot_wizard(
    robot_name="franka_panda",
    dest_path="/World/Franka1",
    position=[-1.0, 0, 0.75],
    orientation=[0.7071068, 0, 0, 0.7071068],
)

# Robot 2 — picks from Conv2 (y=-0.4), drops in Bin (y=+0.4)
# Orientation -90° around Z so Conv2 at world -Y is robot's base +X
robot_wizard(
    robot_name="franka_panda",
    dest_path="/World/Franka2",
    position=[+1.0, 0, 0.75],
    orientation=[0.7071068, 0, 0, -0.7071068],
)

# Conv1 — input belt, carries cube +X to Robot 1's pick zone at x=-1
create_conveyor(
    prim_path="/World/Conv1",
    position=[-1.5, 0.4, 0.78],
    size=[3.0, 0.4, 0.05],
    surface_velocity=[0.2, 0, 0],
)

# Conv2 — transfer belt, carries cube +X from Robot 1's drop to Robot 2's pick at x=+1
create_conveyor(
    prim_path="/World/Conv2",
    position=[0.0, -0.4, 0.78],
    size=[3.0, 0.4, 0.05],
    surface_velocity=[0.2, 0, 0],
)

# One cube starts at far left of Conv1
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Cube_1", prim_type="Cube", position=[-2.5, 0.4, 0.835], size=0.05)
for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
    apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Cube_1", schema_name=api)

# sleepThreshold=0 so cube is never deactivated by PhysX while belt is paused
bulk_set_attribute(
    prim_paths=["/World/Cube_1"],
    attr="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold",
    value=0.0,
)

# Rubber friction material on cube only (combined-friction with default fingers)
apply_physics_material(prim_path="/World/Cube_1", material_name="rubber")

# Bin near Robot 2 (drops here at end of pipeline)
create_bin(prim_path="/World/Bin", position=[1.0, 0.4, 0.75], size=[0.3, 0.3, 0.15])

# Sensors at each robot's pick zone
add_proximity_sensor(sensor_path="/World/Sensor1", position=[-1.0, 0.4, 0.835], size=[0.06, 0.06, 0.06])
add_proximity_sensor(sensor_path="/World/Sensor2", position=[1.0, -0.4, 0.835], size=[0.06, 0.06, 0.06])

# Robot 1 controller — destination_path=Conv2 (so _is_in_bin succeeds when cube on Conv2)
# drop_target explicit because Conv2's bbox center is out of Robot 1's reach.
# Drop z=0.94: hand at 0.94, fingertips at 0.835, cube center released ~1cm above belt top.
setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path="/World/Franka1",
    target_source="curobo",
    sensor_path="/World/Sensor1",
    belt_path="/World/Conv1",
    source_paths=["/World/Cube_1"],
    destination_path="/World/Conv2",
    drop_target=[-1.0, -0.4, 0.94],
    planning_obstacles=["/World/Table", "/World/Conv1", "/World/Conv2", "/World/Bin"],
)

# Robot 2 controller — picks Cube_1 from Conv2, drops in Bin
setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path="/World/Franka2",
    target_source="curobo",
    sensor_path="/World/Sensor2",
    belt_path="/World/Conv2",
    source_paths=["/World/Cube_1"],
    destination_path="/World/Bin",
    planning_obstacles=["/World/Table", "/World/Conv1", "/World/Conv2", "/World/Bin"],
)
failure_modes: Both robots calling setup_pick_place_controller with the old _SUB_ATTR='_curobo_pp_sub' (un-tagged) → second install unsubscribes first robot's controller. Fix landed in 2026-05-06 commit: handler now scopes _SUB_ATTR by ROBOT_PATH so installs coexist.

Robot 1 destination_path=Conv2 without an explicit drop_target → cuRobo plans to Conv2's bbox center (0, -0.4) which is 1.08m from Robot 1 base at (-1, 0) → planning fails forever. Symptom: error_count increments rapidly, robot stuck in 'settling' state. Fix: pass drop_target as a position in robot's reach AND inside the destination prim's xy bbox.

drop_target z too low → panda_hand descends so fingers contact / push cube into the belt before opening. Symptom: cube velocity spike, finger drives stalling. Industrial sweet spot is 5-15mm clearance above belt top; z = belt_top + 0.105 (finger length) + clearance.

Listing the SAME cube in both robots' source_paths but using different destination_paths is the architectural pattern — each robot has its own S['delivered'] keyed on its own destination's bbox check. Don't try to share state.
Forgetting to set robot 2's orientation → robot reaches in wrong direction → can't pick from Conv2 at y=-0.4. Mirror robot 1's orientation by negating w, z components.

Bin at (1, +0.4): robot 2 reaches it via base -X (joint 1 swings 180°). cuRobo's self_collision_check handles the wrap. If joint limits prevent it, switch bin to a position in robot 2's natural front (-Y direction).

Cube path collision with conveyor surfaces during transit: cuRobo without scene-collision (Warp 1.8 limitation) can route arm through belt geometry. Mitigated by mid-height waypoints in handler's _build_segments — they force near-vertical pick/drop.

Forgetting to apply PhysxRigidBodyAPI to cube before bulk_set_attribute on physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold → attribute exists but PhysX doesn't read it without the typed schema → cube falls asleep on paused belt → never moves when belt resumes.

verified_metrics.controllers: two cuRobo controllers, per-robot subscription names
verified_metrics.delivery: Cube_1 traverses Conv1 → Robot 1 → Conv2 → Robot 2 → Bin end-to-end
verified_metrics.drop_clearance_above_belt_top: 10mm (industrial sweet spot for gripper-pick-place with 5cm cubes)
verified_metrics.tools_only: no run_usd_script — pure tool calls verified end-to-end
verified_metrics.open_known_issues: test-harness builtins cleanup between repeat runs uses a kit_tools.exec_sync — not part of canonical pattern (agent starts fresh)

extension_notes: CP-02 reuses CP-01's pick-place mechanics (cuRobo controller, settling state, mid-waypoints, friction material, sleepThreshold=0) but adds two architectural requirements: (a) per-robot subscription naming so multiple cuRobo controllers coexist, (b) explicit drop_target when destination's bbox center is out of robot's reach. Both landed in the 2026-05-06 commits.
